parameters.command:
=# 1. Setup paths
BASE_DIR="/data/output"
INPUT_DIR="$BASE_DIR/PDFs"
OUTPUT_DIR="$BASE_DIR/Compressed_PDFs"
ARCHIVE_DIR="$BASE_DIR/Archive_PDFs"

mkdir -p "$INPUT_DIR" "$OUTPUT_DIR" "$ARCHIVE_DIR"

# 2. Find newest PDF
PDF_FILE=$(ls -t "$INPUT_DIR"/*.pdf "$INPUT_DIR"/*.PDF 2>/dev/null | head -1)

if [ -z "$PDF_FILE" ]; then
  echo "{\"status\": \"error\", \"msg\": \"No PDF found in $INPUT_DIR.\"}"
  exit 1
fi

BASENAME=$(basename "$PDF_FILE")
OUTPUT_FILE="$OUTPUT_DIR/$BASENAME"
ARCHIVE_FILE="$ARCHIVE_DIR/$BASENAME"

# 3. Compress PDF
COMPRESS_SUCCESS=false

if command -v gs >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Ghostscript is preferred for best compression (/ebook preset ~150dpi)
  if gs -sDEVICE=pdfwrite -dCompatibilityLevel=1.4 -dPDFSETTINGS=/ebook -dNOPAUSE -dQUIET -dBATCH -sOutputFile="$OUTPUT_FILE" "$PDF_FILE"; then
    COMPRESS_SUCCESS=true
    TOOL_USED="ghostscript"
  fi
elif command -v mutool >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # Fallback to mutool clean (with garbage collection and compression)
  if mutool clean -gggg -z "$PDF_FILE" "$OUTPUT_FILE"; then
    COMPRESS_SUCCESS=true
    TOOL_USED="mutool"
  fi
fi

if [ "$COMPRESS_SUCCESS" = true ]; then
  # Move original to archive
  mv "$PDF_FILE" "$ARCHIVE_FILE"
  
  # Calculate sizes
  ORIG_SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$ARCHIVE_FILE")
  NEW_SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$OUTPUT_FILE")
  
  echo "{\"status\": \"success\", \"inputFile\": \"$BASENAME\", \"toolUsed\": \"$TOOL_USED\", \"originalSizeBytes\": $ORIG_SIZE, \"newSizeBytes\": $NEW_SIZE}"
else
  echo "{\"status\": \"error\", \"msg\": \"Compression failed. Neither ghostscript nor mutool succeeded or are installed.\"}"
  exit 1
fi
